figures.caption: Fig. 3: AUROC matrices for the 5 actions from each dataset with target action (action data for learning assessment mode) in y-axis and query action (data used for evaluating assessment model) in x-axis.

Fig. 2: Architecture of parser. We follow an encoder-decoder setup where we learn an ST-GCN [51] encoder to capture the spatio-temporal dependencies of the MoCap data and fine-tune Qwen2.5-Coder-3B [21] decoder using LoRA [20]. For training we use loss in Eq. 6.

Table 1: Segmental F1 scores (↑) for human action segmentation experiments averaged over 3 runs. The results in bold indicate the best-performing segmentation model, with a statistically significant difference (t-test).

Table 2: PR-AUC (↑) for human action segmentation experiments averaged over 3 runs. The results in bold indicate the best-performing segmentation model, with a statistically significant difference (t-test).

Fig. 1: Overview of executable action modelling for human action understanding. Parser is trained from motion-capture (MoCap) data to ExAct motion program by leveraging a program-conditioned generative model to create synthetic training data. It is used in model building to aggregate ExAct programs from human MoCap data based on their action labels.
